strengths: Decent hand-eye coordination to make clean contact
Stance width is stable at setup
improvements: Extremely short and flat backswing limits power potential
Lack of shoulder turn and hip rotation in the backswing
Loss of balance on the follow-through, stepping forward with the trail foot
Rushed tempo with an abrupt transition
analysis.key_fault: Extremely abbreviated backswing and lack of body rotation, resulting in an arm-dominated swing and poor balance.

drill_1.name: L-to-L Drill
drill_1.description: Focus on hinging your wrists to create an 'L' shape between your lead arm and the club shaft halfway back, and mirror this 'L' shape on the follow-through. This helps lengthen the swing and improve wrist hinge.

drill_2.name: Shoulder Turn Drill
drill_2.description: Place a club across your shoulders and practice turning until the lead end of the club points behind the ball. This trains a full shoulder turn and prevents a short, arm-only backswing.

drill_3.name: Hold Your Finish Drill
drill_3.description: Make swings focusing on holding your finish completely balanced on your lead side for 3 seconds. This will prevent you from falling backward or stepping out of the swing.

analysis.summary: Your swing is currently very short and dominated by the arms, which limits your power and causes you to lose balance on the follow-through. By focusing on a fuller shoulder turn and proper wrist hinge (L-to-L drill), you will create a wider, more powerful arc and improve your overall consistency.
